channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: DOJ hides Trump-related Epstein files. And, what to know for the State of the Union
item.description: An NPR investigation found that the DOJ withheld some Epstein files related to abuse allegations against Trump. And, what you need to know ahead of the president's State of the Union address tonight.
item.content:encoded:
An NPR investigation found that the DOJ withheld some Epstein files related to abuse allegations against Trump. And, what you need to know ahead of the president's State of the Union address tonight.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Democrats tap Spanberger and Padilla to respond to State of the Union
item.description: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger will deliver Democrats' response on Tuesday following President Trump's State of the Union address.
item.content:encoded:
Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger will deliver Democrats' response on Tuesday following President Trump's State of the Union address.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Is the YIMBY movement doomed?
item.description: For decades, rising home prices have been an engine for middle-class wealth. Now a growing movement wants to slow — or even reverse — that trend. Are the politics around new housing development inherently stacked against them?
item.content:encoded:
For decades, rising home prices have been an engine for middle-class wealth. Now a growing movement wants to slow — or even reverse — that trend. Are the politics around new housing development inherently stacked against them?

(Image credit: Matt Cardy)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: What you need to know as Russia's full-scale war on Ukraine enters its 5th year
item.description: Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine four years ago, and the fighting continues. Here's a look at where the war stands today.
item.content:encoded:
Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine four years ago, and the fighting continues. Here's a look at where the war stands today.

(Image credit: Vitalii Nosach)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: A U.S. veteran adopted an orphan from Iran. Decades later, ICE is trying to deport her
item.description: The woman has no criminal record and is unsure what prompted the threat of removal. She fears being deported to Iran given her father's military service and her Christian faith.
item.content:encoded:
The woman has no criminal record and is unsure what prompted the threat of removal. She fears being deported to Iran given her father's military service and her Christian faith.

(Image credit: Stella Kalinina for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: What you need to know about tonight's State of the Union address
item.description: The prime-time address is a chance for the president to tout his record ahead of this year's midterm elections. But it comes at a moment when Trump has seen his agenda complicated on multiple fronts.
item.content:encoded:
The prime-time address is a chance for the president to tout his record ahead of this year's midterm elections. But it comes at a moment when Trump has seen his agenda complicated on multiple fronts.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: 'Fear of Flying Clinic' helps anxious travelers back into the skies
item.description: For 50 years, a San Francisco-based group has created a space where fearful flyers can get supported exposure to air travel.
item.content:encoded:
For 50 years, a San Francisco-based group has created a space where fearful flyers can get supported exposure to air travel.

(Image credit: Evan Roberts)
item.dc:creator: Evan Roberts

item.title: These small business owners are owed tariff refunds. Will they ever get them?
item.description: Anyone who paid the taxes should get reimbursed, but the high court did not address how. Business owners wonder if they'll need lawyers, brokers, money — or luck.
item.content:encoded:
Anyone who paid the taxes should get reimbursed, but the high court did not address how. Business owners wonder if they'll need lawyers, brokers, money — or luck.

(Image credit: ASM Games)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Justice Department withheld and removed some Epstein files related to Trump
item.description: An NPR investigation finds the public database of Epstein files is missing dozens of pages related to sexual abuse accusations against President Trump.
item.content:encoded:
An NPR investigation finds the public database of Epstein files is missing dozens of pages related to sexual abuse accusations against President Trump.

(Image credit: Department of Justice and Getty Images/Collage by Danielle A. Scruggs/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Why Gavin Newsom refuses to be a 'bystander' in this political moment
item.description: What does the Democratic leader see for himself in the years to come?
item.content:encoded: What does the Democratic leader see for himself in the years to come?
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta
